root_text.sn47.23:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca ānando āyasmā ca bhaddo pāṭaliputte viharanti kukkuṭārāme.
root_text.sn47.23:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā bhaddo sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn47.23:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā bhaddo āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.23:1.4: “ko nu kho, āvuso ānanda, hetu, ko paccayo yena saddhammaparihānaṁ hoti?
root_text.sn47.23:1.5: Ko nu kho, āvuso ānanda, hetu, ko paccayo yena saddhammaaparihānaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn47.23:2.2: Bhaddako kho te, āvuso bhadda, ummaṅgo, bhaddakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, kalyāṇī paripucchā.
root_text.sn47.23:2.4: ‘ko nu kho, āvuso ānanda, hetu, ko paccayo yena saddhammaparihānaṁ hoti?
root_text.sn47.23:2.5: Ko panāvuso ānanda, hetu, ko paccayo yena saddhammaaparihānaṁ hotī’”ti?
root_text.sn47.23:2.7: “Catunnaṁ kho, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ abhāvitattā abahulīkatattā saddhammaparihānaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn47.23:2.8: Catunnañca kho, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā saddhammaaparihānaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn47.23:3.2: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.23:3.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.23:3.6: Imesaṁ kho, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ abhāvitattā abahulīkatattā saddhammaparihānaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn47.23:3.7: Imesañca kho, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā saddhammaaparihānaṁ hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn47.23:1.1: At one time the venerables Ānanda and Bhadda were staying near Pāṭaliputta, in the Chicken Monastery.
translation_text.sn47.23:1.2: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Bhadda came out of retreat, went to Venerable Ānanda, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn47.23:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Ānanda:
translation_text.sn47.23:1.4: “What’s the cause, Reverend Ānanda, what’s the reason why the true teaching declines?
translation_text.sn47.23:1.5: And what’s the cause, what’s the reason why the true teaching doesn’t decline?”
translation_text.sn47.23:2.2: Your approach and articulation are excellent, and it’s a good question.
translation_text.sn47.23:2.4: ‘What’s the cause, what’s the reason why the true teaching declines?
translation_text.sn47.23:2.5: And what’s the cause, what’s the reason why the true teaching doesn’t decline?’”
translation_text.sn47.23:2.7: “It’s because of not developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching declines.
translation_text.sn47.23:2.8: It’s because of developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching doesn’t decline.
translation_text.sn47.23:3.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.23:3.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.23:3.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.23:3.6: It’s because of not developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching declines.
translation_text.sn47.23:3.7: And it’s because of developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that the true teaching doesn’t decline.”
